name: Chocolate Silk
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine the sugar, cocoa powder, cornstarch, and gelatin, pressing out all lumps.
2. Add the half-and-half, evaporated skim milk, and whole milk; whisk vigorously to blend until smooth.
3. Set the pan over moderately low heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture just boils and is thickened and smooth, about 10 minutes.
4. Blend a small amount of the hot mixture into the beaten egg yolks.
5. Stir the warmed egg yolks back into the saucepan and cook for 3 to 4 minutes more over moderately low heat, stirring constantly. Do not boil; eggs will scramble if boiled.
6. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla extract.
7. Set the saucepan on a rack to cool for 10 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent a skin from forming on the surface.
8. Ladle the mixture into 6 decorative serving dishes or white porcelain ramekins and let cool completely.
9. Cover each dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 5 hours, or overnight.
10. Serve as is or top each serving with a little whipped cream and either chopped pistachios or a single candied violet.
